articles.headline: Tottenham's Igor Tudor: Relegation is 'life or death' situation
articles.description: Igor Tudor has not spoken about relegation with his Tottenham squad but accepts they face a "life and death" situation in the Premier League.

images.name: Igor Tudor [1296x729]
images.credit: Photo by Rob Newell - CameraSport via Getty Images

images.name: Are Spurs in danger of relegation?
images.caption: The "ESPN FC" crew discuss Tottenham's spot at the bottom of the table.

articles.headline: Weekend predictions: Arsenal, Bayern title bids on the line?
articles.description: It's Friday and another full weekend of action awaits across the soccer calendar as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe.

images.name: Are Arsenal still favourites to win the Premier League?
images.caption: Gab & Juls discuss the Premier League title race between Arsenal and Manchester City.

articles.headline: Liverpool back in profit, but wage bill was highest in Premier League
articles.description: Liverpool returned to profit after their Premier League-winning season with a pre-tax surplus of £15.2million ($20.5) but had the highest wage bill.

images.name: Laurens: Liverpool need to improve if they want a UCL spot
images.caption: Gab & Juls react to Liverpool's late 1-0 win over Nottingham Forest in the Premier League.

articles.headline: Liverpool's Arne Slot backs Mohamed Salah to end drought: 'He always scores in the end'
articles.description: Arne Slot has backed Liverpool forward Mohamed Salah to rediscover his goalscoring form, insisting "he always scores in the end."

images.name: Salah and Slot [1296x729]
images.caption: Liverpool manager Arne Slot and Mohamed Salah seem to be on the same page again, since the striker's return from the Africa Cup of Nations.
images.alt: Liverpool's Egyptian striker #11 Mohamed Salah (R) shakes hands with Liverpool's Dutch manager Arne Slot (L) as he is substituted during the English Premier League football match between Liverpool and Newcastle United at Anfield in Liverpool, north west E
images.credit: Paul ELLIS / AFP via Getty Images

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United, Chelsea, PSG watch Genk trio
articles.description: Scouts from clubs including Chelsea, Manchester City, Man United and Paris Saint-Germain watched three Racing Genk stars play. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Genk's Matte Smets (L) and Dinamo Zagreb forward #71 Monsef Bakrar fight for the ball  [1296x729]
images.caption: Genk's Matte Smets (L) and Dinamo Zagreb forward #71 Monsef Bakrar fight for the ball
images.credit: Johan Eyckens / Belga / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Anthony Gordon [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Serena Taylor/Newcastle United via Getty Images)

images.name: Should Martin Ødegaard leave Arsenal in the summer?
images.caption: The 'FC TV' crew debate if Martin Ødegaard should leave Arsenal in the summer transfer window.

articles.headline: Premier League Power Rankings: Chelsea up, Liverpool down based on performance
articles.description: ESPN's Premier League Power Rankings return to look at all 20 teams: Chelsea are up, Liverpool are down, and Crystal Palace are on fire.

images.name: Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur Michael Carrick Kobbie Mainoo [1296x864]
Marcotti: The time taken for verdict of Man City's 115 charges is absurd
images.caption: Gab Marcotti explains why there has still been no verdict for Manchester City's 115 financial charges.
